puzzles.title: Back Rank Mate
puzzles.explanation: Black's king is trapped behind its own pawns. Ra8# delivers mate along the open back rank.

puzzles.title: Queen to the Back Rank
puzzles.explanation: The e-file is wide open. Qe8# checks along the back rank with no escape squares for the king.

puzzles.title: Smothered Mate
puzzles.explanation: Black's king is completely smothered by its own rook and pawns. Nf7# is checkmate because the king has nowhere to go and nothing can capture the knight.

puzzles.title: Ladder Mate
puzzles.explanation: The rook on a7 cuts off the 7th rank. Rb8# delivers mate on the back rank with the king unable to step onto rank 7.

puzzles.title: Punish the Greedy Knight
puzzles.explanation: Black ignored the threat to f7. Qxf7# is checkmate — the queen is defended by the bishop on c4 and the king has no escape squares.

puzzles.title: Royal Fork
puzzles.explanation: Ne6+ forks the king on g7 and the queen on d8. After the king moves, White wins the queen.

puzzles.title: Knight Fork: King and Rook
puzzles.explanation: Nd6+ forks the king on e8 and the rook on c8. The king must move, then White captures the rook.

puzzles.title: Pawn Fork
puzzles.explanation: d5! attacks both the knight on c6 and the bishop on e6 with a single pawn move. One of them will fall.

puzzles.title: Queen Fork
puzzles.explanation: Qb8+ checks the king and simultaneously attacks the rook on a8 — a winning fork.

puzzles.title: Pin the Knight
puzzles.explanation: Bb5 pins the knight on c6 to the king on e8 — the knight cannot move without exposing the king to check. The bishop also attacks the now-frozen, undefended knight directly, so Bxc6 wins it next move.

puzzles.title: Pin the Knight to the Queen
puzzles.explanation: Rd1 pins the knight on d5 to the queen on d8. If the knight moves, the rook wins the queen.

puzzles.title: Diagonal Pin
puzzles.explanation: Qa4 pins the bishop on c6 to the king on e8 along the long a4-e8 diagonal. The bishop has no defender, so it is now stuck and lost — Qxc6 wins it next move.

puzzles.title: Back Rank Skewer
puzzles.explanation: Ra8+ skewers the king on c8. After the king moves off the back rank, the rook captures the rook on h8.

puzzles.title: Bishop Skewer
puzzles.explanation: Bf7+ skewers the king on d5 and the queen on a2 along the long diagonal. Once the king moves, the bishop wins the queen.

puzzles.title: Discovered Check
puzzles.explanation: Nb5+ uncovers an attack from the rook on a1 to the king on a8 (discovered check) while the knight itself attacks the queen on d6.

puzzles.title: Unveil the Rook
puzzles.explanation: Be3 steps off the d-file, revealing the rook on d1's attack on the undefended queen on d8 — while the bishop itself now attacks the knight on b6, which only the queen was defending. Black cannot save both pieces.

puzzles.title: Queen Double Attack
puzzles.explanation: Qa5 attacks the rook on a8 along the file and the knight on h5 along the rank at the same time.

puzzles.title: Rook Double Attack
puzzles.explanation: Rd4 attacks the bishop on d7 along the file and the knight on b4 along the rank at the same time.

puzzles.title: Queen and Knight Mate
puzzles.explanation: Qg8# is checkmate: the knight on f6 defends the queen and covers g8, while the queen itself covers g7 and h7.

puzzles.title: Back Rank Mate with Bishop Support
puzzles.explanation: Ra8# is mate — the bishop on b2 covers the only escape square on g7.
